explicit_text: I came to Boynton in 1907 from DeLand, Florida, and have lived here ever since. Back then, Boynton was mostly hard living with only a few good homes, and it wasn't even incorporated as Boynton Beach yet. My husband and I started farming tomatoes here, renting land at first and then buying seven and a half acres near the Gulf Stream Clubhouse. Over the years, we made this place our home and community, contributing to its early development.
implicit_text: I came to this coastal Florida town in 1907 from DeLand, Florida, and have lived here ever since. My husband and I started farming tomatoes here, renting land at first and then buying seven and a half acres near the local clubhouse by the ocean. Over the years, we made this place our home and community, contributing to its early development.

explicit_text: Freda Oyer is one of Boynton's best-known long-time residents, just like me and Glen Murray. We all have been part of this community for many years, witnessing its growth and changes from the early 1900s. Although I don't share many personal stories about her, I know she is recognized alongside me for our long-standing presence in Boynton.

explicit_text: I was born in DeLand, Florida, in 1887, just three years after the town was surveyed. It was a rural area, what people called 'the sticks,' and my father was a citrus grower and cattle man there. I lived in DeLand until I was twenty before moving to Boynton in 1907 with my husband. DeLand was truly my childhood home and shaped much of my early life.
implicit_text: I was born in a small city in central Florida in 1887, just three years after the town was surveyed. It was a rural area, what people called 'the sticks,' and my father was a citrus grower and cattle man there. I lived in that city until I was twenty before moving to the coastal town in 1907 with my husband. That city was truly my childhood home and shaped much of my early life.

explicit_text: My father was born in Georgia, and his father moved to Florida when he was about three years old. Although I was born and raised in Florida, my family roots trace back to Georgia through my father. This connection to Georgia is part of my family's migration story into Florida.
implicit_text: My father was born in a southern U.S. state known for its peaches and historic cities, and his father moved to Florida when he was about three years old. Although I was born and raised in Florida, my family roots trace back to that southern state through my father. This connection is part of my family's migration story into Florida.

explicit_text: Volusia County is where I was born, specifically in DeLand, and also where my mother was from, in DeLeon Springs. Later, my husband and I tried to buy a turpentine business in Volusia County, but it got tied up in court for years. Despite that, Volusia County was an important part of my early life and family history.

Despite that, this county was an important part of my early life and family history.

explicit_text: My mother was born in DeLeon Springs, Florida, which is in Volusia County. This place is part of my family heritage, connecting me to the area before I moved to Boynton. Although I left DeLand and DeLeon Springs when I was twenty, these places remain important to my family story.

explicit_text: Henry Flagler's association with the east coast influenced my great uncle and his son to settle near what is now Florida City, where they farmed successfully. Their connection to Flagler helped them establish a prosperous farming business, which indirectly influenced my husband JJ's decision to explore opportunities in South Florida. Flagler's impact on the region was significant in shaping early settlement patterns.
implicit_text: A famous industrialist associated with Florida's east coast rail development influenced my great uncle and his son to settle near what is now a southern Florida city, where they farmed successfully. Their connection to this industrialist helped them establish a prosperous farming business, which indirectly influenced my husband JJ's decision to explore opportunities in South Florida. His impact on the region was significant in shaping early settlement patterns.

explicit_text: My great uncle and his son settled in Florida City, farming there with good success. JJ visited them to see if we might move there, but they warned us about the harsh living conditions and insects, saying it was no place for me. So although Florida City was promising, we decided against moving there and instead came to Boynton.
implicit_text: My great uncle and his son settled in a southern Florida city, farming there with good success. So although that city was promising, we decided against moving there and instead came to the coastal town.

explicit_text: John Jackson, whom I always called JJ, was my husband and partner in all our ventures. He had been in the turpentine business before we moved to Boynton in 1907. JJ was the one who bought our first Ford car in 1908 and later ran a taxi service to West Palm Beach. He was instrumental in our farming and business success here.

JJ was the one who bought our first Ford car in 1908 and later ran a taxi service to the nearby city. He was instrumental in our farming and business success here.

explicit_text: Byrd S. Dewey was one of the prominent pioneers in Boynton when I arrived in 1907. He owned a very nice home and his name is on many things in Boynton because he helped subdivide the town. His presence marked the early development of Boynton, and I remember him as part of the community's foundation.

explicit_text: Our farming land in Boynton was located just one block from the Gulf Stream Club clubhouse, near A1A. This club was a notable landmark in the area, and our property stretched from the ocean to the canal. The Gulf Stream Club was part of the early landscape of Boynton Beach as it developed.
implicit_text: Our farming land in the coastal town was located just one block from the clubhouse of a notable local club near the main coastal highway. This club was a notable landmark in the area, and our property stretched from the ocean to the canal. The club was part of the early landscape of the town as it developed.

explicit_text: Delray was the neighboring town to Boynton, with its city limits adjoining the acreage we bought. The first acre south of our land was owned by Delray people, and the two communities were closely connected even though neither was incorporated at the time. Delray and Boynton shared a rural character in those early years.

explicit_text: West Palm Beach was the nearest city to Boynton, and before we had cars, people traveled there by boat or train. When the Boynton Hotel opened, JJ and I started a taxi service to West Palm Beach to help hotel guests and locals get around. This service was quite successful and made transportation easier for many in the area.
implicit_text: West Palm Beach was the nearest city to the coastal town, and before we had cars, people traveled there by boat or train. explicit_text: On our first automobile trip from Boynton to DeLand in 1908, we passed through Titusville, where I noticed they had one street with rock paving. The journey was difficult, taking two days and nights because of sand beds and mechanical troubles. Titusville was one of the few places with any kind of paved road back then.
implicit_text: On our first automobile trip from the coastal town to the central Florida city in 1908, we passed through a city on the east coast where I noticed they had one street with rock paving. The journey was difficult, taking two days and nights because of sand beds and mechanical troubles. That city was one of the few places with any kind of paved road back then.

explicit_text: JJ bought me a Ford passenger car for my birthday in 1908, which was quite an event since there were no paved roads and travel was difficult. The car was purchased from George Barco in West Palm Beach, and it became our main mode of transportation. This Ford car even allowed us to start a taxi service in Boynton, which was successful for many years.

This purchase was significant for us because it opened up new opportunities for transportation and business in Boynton. I remember the trip back to DeLand was challenging, but having the car was a big step forward.

explicit_text: I was involved with the Eastern Star and attended meetings regularly. I recall going to an Eastern Star meeting in Delray, which was part of my social life in the area. The organization provided community and fellowship during those early years in Boynton and nearby towns.
